description: Machine-readable Lenia organism parameter catalog. Each entry is a complete configuration sufficient to seed the simulation. Feed directly into AI generators or use as-is in lenia.js / lenia_5ch.js.

organisms.name: Orbium
organisms.behavior: Smooth gliding sphere. The hydrogen atom of Lenia — the simplest stable organism every researcher finds first. Speed ~0.5 px/step. Perfectly round. Splits symmetrically if two collide head-on.
organisms.visual: Dense white-hot magenta core, dark halo ring, clean straight wake. Looks like a living cell under electron microscope.

organisms.name: Scutium
organisms.behavior: Flattened shield-shape. Faster than Orbium (~0.8 px/step). Visible asymmetric density distribution — front hemisphere is brighter and denser than rear.
organisms.visual: Oblate oval, leading edge lit like a crescent moon, trailing edge fades. Scythe-shaped wake.

organisms.name: Gyrorbium
organisms.behavior: Translates AND rotates. The second ring in the kernel breaks the symmetry enough to induce spin. Leaves a helical wake. Rotation period ~30 steps.
organisms.visual: Sphere with a dimmer outer ring, comet-like spiral wake that slowly corkscrews across the field.

organisms.name: Aquarium
organisms.behavior: Dense stationary blob with pulsing membrane. No locomotion, but visible surface oscillation. Looks like a breathing cell membrane under stress.
organisms.visual: Bright white core, thick diffuse halo. Membrane boundary shimmers with standing-wave ripples at 5–8 frames period.

organisms.name: Hydrogeminium
organisms.behavior: Unstable at startup. Organizes over ~500 frames into a stable swimmer. At high population density, undergoes binary fission — splits into two viable daughter organisms. The only single-organism system that reproduces without engineered seeding.
organisms.visual: Turbulent magenta cloud condensing into an elongated form. Fission event: single organism elongates into a barbell shape, then pinches. Two daughters diverge.

organisms.name: Chromorbium
organisms.behavior: Three channels pulse at offset phases — the organism appears to breathe. RGB composite shows color cycling from magenta to cyan to violet as internal oscillation propagates through the connection matrix.
organisms.visual: Shifting chromatic membrane. When Ch0 peaks (magenta), Ch1 (cyan) is in its growth phase. Ch2 (violet) lags by ~15 frames. The organism looks like it has a beating, color-changing heart.

organisms.name: Volvox
organisms.behavior: Multiple sub-organisms orbit a shared center of mass. Looks like a rotating colony of microbes. The rotation is emergent from channel-channel coupling — not programmed. Colony period ~120–200 frames depending on initial conditions.
organisms.visual: 4–6 bright lobes orbiting a dim center. Trails from each lobe create a pinwheel of glowing cyan and magenta arcs. The whole structure looks like a living mandala.

organisms.name: Pentarbium
organisms.behavior: Five-channel organism with visible internal structure — distinct 'organs' visible in channel overlay. Ch4 acts as a structural scaffold, making the organism's skeleton visible as a dim geometric armature.
organisms.visual: Complex multi-lobed organism. Channel overlay: magenta core, cyan excitation cloud, violet suppression halo, orange trace trail, and a faint white skeletal frame that holds the whole shape together.

failure_modes.name: White noise explosion
failure_modes.visual: System explodes into uniform white noise in 10–20 frames. Aesthetically: instant static art. Render it.

failure_modes.name: Gray fog
failure_modes.visual: Organism dissolves into homogeneous gray field. No structure. Slow fade to #808080.

failure_modes.name: Exponential saturation
failure_modes.visual: Full field saturates to 1.0 in seconds. Renders as solid white-hot bloom. Then slowly decays if you re-enable inhibition.

failure_modes.name: Two-speed interference
failure_modes.visual: Fast-scale and slow-scale dynamics fight each other. Produces Moiré-like interference rings and turbulent, spinning glitch-organisms.
